response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the resume shows no exposure to the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design. The background is almost entirely application-layer software (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang web services), not silicon performance architecture. Educational background lacks a graduate degree in a hardware-relevant field, and there is no demonstrated principal-level hardware architecture experience.
strengths: Strong polyglot software engineering background across many years and domains
Some low-level/high-performance experience with DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec
Brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No demonstrated deep-learning workload analysis or hardware-software co-design background
Educational and career path does not match principal-level hardware performance architecture requirements

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload optimization background
